title: pinus mugo subsp. mughus
intro: Pinus mugo subsp. mughus, also known as Mugo Pine, is a subspecies of evergreen coniferous tree native to the European Alps. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and has been used to treat various health issues such as respiratory problems, wounds, and skin conditions. The essential oil extracted from the plant's needles has been shown to have health benefits, including reducing stress, improving circulation, and boosting the immune system. Pinus mugo subsp. mughus can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the needles in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
